gaps.title: Cache Invalidation Race Condition
gaps.breaks: Stale cached responses returned after model updates due to timing issues
gaps.scenario: When updating a model configuration while concurrent requests are hitting the cache, some requests get the old cached response instead of the new one
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent cache writes and reads with model updates. Verify that after a model configuration change, no stale responses are returned. Use Redis transactions and check for race conditions.

gaps.title: Cost Calculation Inconsistency
gaps.breaks: Incorrect cost tracking when cached responses are served multiple times
gaps.scenario: A cached response is served 10 times but costs are only calculated once, leading to underreported costs and potential budget limit bypass
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies cost tracking is accurate for both fresh requests and cached responses. Ensure costs are properly tracked each time a cached response is served and that budget limits are enforced correctly.

gaps.title: Memory Leak with Large Responses
gaps.breaks: Redis memory exhaustion when large responses exceed TTL cleanup
gaps.scenario: A tenant with very large AI responses (e.g., 10MB each) fills Redis memory before TTL cleanup can occur, causing system-wide performance degradation
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates serving large responses repeatedly and monitors Redis memory usage. Implement and test a solution that prevents memory exhaustion while maintaining cache functionality.

gaps.title: Tenant Isolation in Cache
gaps.breaks: Cross-tenant data leakage in shared Redis cache
gaps.scenario: Due to improper key naming, tenant A's cached response is returned to tenant B when they request the same prompt
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in the Redis cache. Ensure all cache keys include proper tenant identifiers and that responses are never shared between tenants.

gaps.title: Cache Stampede Effect
gaps.breaks: Multiple simultaneous requests for same uncached key causing database overload
gaps.scenario: When a popular cache key expires and multiple concurrent requests hit the system simultaneously, all bypass the cache and hit the database, causing cascading failures
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates cache stampede conditions. Implement and test a solution that prevents multiple concurrent requests for the same uncached key from all hitting the database.

gaps.title: Cost Tracking Precision Loss
gaps.breaks: Rounding errors in cost calculation lead to budget limit bypass
gaps.scenario: Multiple small token requests accumulate costs that fall between integer boundaries, allowing a tenant to exceed their budget limit without being stopped
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies cost calculation precision across multiple small requests. Ensure that accumulated costs are properly tracked and that budget limits are enforced with appropriate precision.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 6

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Cache Invalidation Race Condition
What breaks: Stale cached responses returned after model updates due to timing issues
Real scenario: When updating a model configuration while concurrent requests are hitting the cache, some requests get the old cached response instead of the new one
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent cache writes and reads with model updates. Verify that after a model configuration change, no stale responses are returned. Use Redis transactions and check for race conditions.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Cost Calculation Inconsistency
What breaks: Incorrect cost tracking when cached responses are served multiple times
Real scenario: A cached response is served 10 times but costs are only calculated once, leading to underreported costs and potential budget limit bypass
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies cost tracking is accurate for both fresh requests and cached responses. Ensure costs are properly tracked each time a cached response is served and that budget limits are enforced correctly.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Memory Leak with Large Responses
What breaks: Redis memory exhaustion when large responses exceed TTL cleanup
Real scenario: A tenant with very large AI responses (e.g., 10MB each) fills Redis memory before TTL cleanup can occur, causing system-wide performance degradation
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates serving large responses repeatedly and monitors Redis memory usage. Implement and test a solution that prevents memory exhaustion while maintaining cache functionality.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant Isolation in Cache
What breaks: Cross-tenant data leakage in shared Redis cache
Real scenario: Due to improper key naming, tenant A's cached response is returned to tenant B when they request the same prompt
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in the Redis cache. Ensure all cache keys include proper tenant identifiers and that responses are never shared between tenants.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Cache Stampede Effect
What breaks: Multiple simultaneous requests for same uncached key causing database overload
Real scenario: When a popular cache key expires and multiple concurrent requests hit the system simultaneously, all bypass the cache and hit the database, causing cascading failures
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates cache stampede conditions. Implement and test a solution that prevents multiple concurrent requests for the same uncached key from all hitting the database.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Cost Tracking Precision Loss
What breaks: Rounding errors in cost calculation lead to budget limit bypass
Real scenario: Multiple small token requests accumulate costs that fall between integer boundaries, allowing a tenant to exceed their budget limit without being stopped
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies cost calculation precision across multiple small requests. Ensure that accumulated costs are properly tracked and that budget limits are enforced with appropriate precision.
